title: I work in a different state from where I'm registered
subtitle: Understanding your voting rights as a migrant

intro.title: You have voting rights. Let's understand them.
intro.body: Right now, India doesn't yet have a remote voting system that works for migrants. But you do have options. Let's walk through them.

choices.label: I want to keep voting in my home village/town
choices.sublabel: I'll travel back when there's an election

choices.label: I want to register where I work now
choices.sublabel: I've been here a while and don't plan to return

choices.label: I'm not sure — show me both

compare.title: Both have trade-offs.
compare.body:
**Voting at home:**
- Keep your existing registration
- Need to physically travel for elections
- Better if you eventually plan to return

**Registering here:**
- File a fresh Form 6 at current address
- Old registration auto-deleted
- Better if you're settled

There's no remote postal ballot for migrants currently.
The ECI is piloting Remote Voting Machines (RVMs) but they're not deployed yet.

stayHome.title: You're already registered. Plan ahead.
stayHome.body: Track your home constituency's election schedule. Many states announce 5–8 weeks ahead. Plan travel and request leave from your employer.

registerHere.eyebrow: Path B — Register at current address
registerHere.title: You'll file Form 6 at your current address.
registerHere.body:
Address proof options for migrants:
- Aadhaar with current address
- Rent agreement (formal or informal)
- Letter from employer
- Bank passbook with current address
- Utility bill in your name

calendar.title: Add an election watch reminder.
calendar.body: Set a recurring reminder to check your home constituency's election schedule every 6 months.

calendar.title: Check home election schedule (MAI reminder)
calendar.description: Visit eci.gov.in to check upcoming elections in your home constituency

complete.title: You're set up to vote.
complete.summary: Migrant voter rights are improving. Watch the news for Remote Voting Machine pilots from ECI — when launched, they'll let you vote without travel.
